page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m4.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 12 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring no resource sharing at the thread level.

This instance is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the ecs.u2i-c1m4.3xlarge offers predictable compute performance for general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring consistent CPU execution without hyperthreading overhead, such as mid-sized web servers, application backends, containerized microservices, and moderate database workloads that rely on external storage volumes.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m4.3xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon processor operating at 2.7 GHz, providing 12 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance features 48.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, requiring external network-attached storage solutions. Network performance is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This hardware profile makes the instance suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and a balanced memory-to-core ratio, such as application servers, containerized environments, and general-purpose business applications.
